content: We can hypothesize that integrating multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms into the Self-Refine framework can break self-feedback confirmation bias that occurs when a single LLM refines its own output iteratively. This modified multi-perspective Self-Refine would retain the original framework's key advantage of requiring no extra supervised training or reinforcement learning, while boosting output quality beyond what single-perspective self-refinement can achieve.
